elements.text: Adoption has been promising in high-level policy but slow at an instutional level

Progress since 2018
Open Hardware is included in upcoming UNESCO Open Science report
Few institutions have pushed forward major initiatives on open hardware, typically still limited to individual labs (in 2019 over 500 papers were published).
Challenges
For science hardware, the majority of the technology development is still taking place in universities and research institutions. There is a major need to engage university management and TTOs. Funders could play a major role in generating incentives.
Many challenges echoing open data and open software at universities: career paths, incentives, time to document, inclusion in funding, advocacy, credit etc
